title: The prevailing  economic catastrophe may create the conditions for a democratic breakthrough,  if Sri Lanka’s fractured left could harness this  emerging sense of solidarity among Affected people
body_lines: I am not in Sri Lanka, and I feel torn about what is happening there. Acute anxiety about how millions will survive the shortages of food, fuel and medicine jostles against a glimmer of hope that this crisis could be the beginning of the end of a decades-long nightmare.

Since the country gained its Independence in 1948, various sections of the population have been targeted by its ruling bloc: threatened with losing their homes, livelihoods and often their lives. They have fought back, but each section has been isolated and crushed by an increasingly centralized and ruthless state. Now, for the first time, the vast majority of the population has risen in revolt. Criticism of the dictatorship is widespread, and divisions between working people may finally be healing.

It is not easy to disentangle the different strands of Sri Lanka’s long-standing political crisis, but let me try. Equality before the law – a key component of any democratic republic – was never supported by the ruling class that took power in independent Ceylon. The two main parties, the United National Party (UNP) and the Sri Lanka Freedom Party (SLFP), both endorsed ‘Sinhala-Buddhist’ supremacism. This meant persistent discrimination against ethno-religious minorities, which began right after Independence, when the UNP passed legislation disenfranchising around a million Tamils of recent Indian origin and stripping them of their citizenship. Most of those affected were plantation workers in the central hill country, who were already isolated from other sections of the working class by their confinement to the plantations.

The next major assault on equality occurred when the SLFP, led by S.W.R.D. Bandaranaike, came to power in 1956 and passed the Official Language Act, or ‘Sinhala Only Bill’. The Act discriminated against all Tamil-speakers, especially in public sector employment. It sparked major protests followed by the anti-Tamil riots of 1958, in which far-right Buddhist monks played a major part, assassinating Bandaranaike the following year for not going far enough in persecuting Tamils. The leadership of the SLFP was taken over by his widow Sirimavo Bandaranaike. Tamil was downgraded and English ceased to act as a link language, in a deliberate attempt to obstruct dialogue between communities.

Such measures were opposed by the Trotskyist Lanka Sama Samaja Party (LSSP) and the Communist Party of Ceylon (later the Communist Party of Sri Lanka, CPSL). Yet, once they failed to prevent the ratification of the anti-Tamil laws, the left parties did not continue the struggle against discrimination by building solidarity among working people from different linguistic and religious groups.

Instead, they formed an alliance with the SLFP in 1964, and the parties jointly established the United Front (UF), which swept to power in 1970. At that point, principled members of the left parties split off, and Tamil socialists were left demoralized by the capitulation of their leaders. The only force that could have carried through the democratic revolution had splintered.

Once in power, the UF’s Land Reform Laws of 1972 and 1975 nationalized the plantations. Yet rather than distributing the land to Tamil workers – who were driven out and left to starve – it was handed to Sinhalese government supporters. In response, the Tamil United Liberation Front (TULF) called for the creation of an independent Tamil Eelam. Militant groups, most notably the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (LTTE), were established to fight for this goal.

The LTTE initially attracted some Tamil socialists who believed it was waging a justified struggle for self-determination; but, in reality, the group was always committed to creating a Tamil-supremacist state by ethnically cleansing and killing Sinhalese. It even targeted Tamil-speaking Muslims in the Northern and Eastern Provinces which it claimed as its territory.

Despite all the privileges given to the Sinhalese majority by the UF, dissatisfaction with the regime remained widespread. Significant gains in healthcare and education were cancelled out in the public mind by inflation and food shortages. In 1971, the Janatha Vimukthi Peramuna (JVP or People’s Liberation Front), led by Rohana Wijeweera, launched an armed uprising to overthrow the government – backed overwhelmingly by Sinhalese young men for whom the problems of unemployment and poverty had not been solved by Sinhala Only.

Notwithstanding his self-description as a ‘modern Bolshevik’, Wijeweera’s revolutionary horizons were narrowed by a Sinhala-supremacist outlook which characterized Tamil plantation workers as mere tools of Indian expansionism. His uprising was powerless to reach across ethnic lines, and ultimately crushed by the UF government after a state of emergency was declared. Nonetheless, antipathy towards the ruling party lingered. When parliamentary elections were held in 1977, the UNP led by J.R. Jayawardene returned to power with 140 seats out of 168. He used this super-majority to enact a new constitution and anoint himself Executive President – with almost unlimited powers.

Sinhala dominance was entrenched under Jayawardene, with anti-Tamil pogroms sweeping the country just a month after his election. The 1979 Prevention of Terrorism Act precipitated the torture, disappearance and extrajudicial killing of thousands of Tamils. In 1981, an orgy of state-sponsored arson, rape and looting in Jaffna included the burning of the public library, with around 95,000 books and ancient manuscripts inside. The violence then shifted to the east, south and hill-country, with thousands of Tamils evicted from their homes and robbed of their possessions. The even more gruesome massacres of 1983 initiated a civil war between the LTTE and government, which lasted some 26 years.

All this perpetuated the trend set by Jayawardene’s predecessors. But what distinguished his regime was its disastrous neoliberalization programme and unabashed authoritarianism. Production of consumer goods, both agricultural and industrial, was hit by cheaper imports in the 1980s, while the import of luxuries previously unavailable in Sri Lanka added to the drain on foreign exchange. Remittances from migrant workers, tea exports, tourism and new foreign investments failed to fill the gap, due to generous tax holidays and tariff-free imports of inputs. This led to increasing reliance on foreign debt, laying the basis for the current economic crisis.

Meanwhile, Jayawardene attempted to crush all dissent and extinguish democracy. His newly established Jathika Sevaka Sangamaya (JSS) was deployed in anti-Tamil pogroms, as well as killing opposition supporters, judges, trade unionists and striking workers, all with the collusion of the police. The second JVP insurrection, starting in mid-1987 and ending in late 1989, when Wijeweera was captured and executed, left an estimated 40,000-60,000 Sinhalese slaughtered in that period alone. Death squads targeted opponents of Jayawardene and his successor Ranasinghe Premadasa, frequently abducting and torturing them to death. Ranil Wickremesinghe, the current Sri Lankan Prime Minister, was a government minister throughout this period. The current president, Gotabaya Rajapaksa, was an army commander. Both were implicated in the mass murder of Tamils and Sinhalese alike.

Since that period, the Executive Presidency has been subjected to an ongoing tug-of-war. Abolishing it has so far proved elusive, because the courts have ruled that this would require a two-thirds majority in parliament plus an absolute majority in a referendum. Yet more modest reforms have occasionally been passed to restrict the presidency’s power. Under the presidency of Chandrika Kumaratunga, from 1994 to 2005, attacks on democracy declined and the 17th Amendment was instituted, removing the president’s ability to unilaterally appoint people to institutions like the Election Commission and Supreme Court. This tentative progress was then reversed under Mahinda Rajapaksa, as state-backed death squads were revived to target dissidents. In 2009, the LTTE was finally defeated in the civil war’s horrific climax, in which an estimated 40,000 Tamil civilians were killed. In tandem, an 18th constitutional amendment reversed Kumaratunga’s reforms and abolished the presidency’s two-term limit.

Rajapaksa rejected the UN’s demand for an independent investigation into reports of war crimes, framing this as an ‘anti-imperialist’ position. But though his rhetoric played well with some of the electorate, he lost credibility by contributing to the country’s mountain of foreign debt – with new creditors including the Chinese government and private buyers of sovereign bonds. With their popularity in decline, the president and his brother Gotabaya Rajapaksa – who, as Defence Secretary, controlled the intelligence agencies – tried to salvage their careers by scapegoating the Muslim population.

They funded far-right groups of Buddhist monks, using them to violently attack Muslims while mounting an Islamophobic propaganda campaign through state-controlled media. Under the radar, the Rajapaksas also funded Islamist militants to fight against the LTTE – who remained on the government payroll as informants despite credible intelligence that they had been radicalized.

Mahinda Rajapaksa was voted out of office in 2015, having alienated a large section of the Sinhalese population with the scandalous nepotism and corruption of his regime. In his place, ethnic minorities voted for a fragile Good Governance (Yahapalanaya) coalition between SLFP rebel Maithripala Sirisena and Ranil Wickremesinghe.

The new coalition once again curtailed the powers of the president and reinstated the two-term limit with the 19th Amendment. But it, too, fell apart. The final blow to its credibility was the Easter Sunday terrorist attack in 2019, which killed 269 people in locations across the country. As it turned out, the bombings were perpetrated by the very same Islamists that the Rajapaksas had been bankrolling. Subsequent investigations revealed that during the Yahapalanaya regime, members of the terror group, including mastermind Zahran Hashim, continued to be paid and protected from prosecution by officials who remained loyal to Gotabaya. This was despite Hashim’s proclamation of allegiance to ISIS and ample evidence that his followers were accumulating arms and explosives.

Yet, counterintuitively, it was the Rajapaksas who benefited from the Easter Sunday massacre. In the resulting panic, the government was weakened and Gotabaya was able to mount an effective presidential campaign, running as the ‘national security’ candidate. Later that year, he topped the poll with 52% of the vote. An alliance led by the Rajapaksas’ new party, the Sri Lanka Podujana Peramuna, also won the parliamentary elections with a large enough majority to pass the 20th Amendment, reversing the 19th. Gotabaya proceeded to pack the cabinet with family members including Mahinda, who was appointed prime minister.

By this time the economy was already sinking under $51 billion of foreign debt, much of it incurred by the family’s vanity projects and endless siphoning of money out of the country. The Gotabaya regime’s tax cuts made the debt unsustainable, and an overnight ban on imports of chemical fertiliser – implemented in the face of farmer protests – led to a colossal decline in crop yields. As foreign reserves ran out, domestic production of food and exports plummeted, leading to escalating unemployment, sky-rocketing inflation, power cuts and long queues to buy basic goods.

All but the very rich have been affected by this meltdown. Workers have lost jobs, farmers are in crisis and fishermen have no fuel to power their boats. Galloping inflation has eroded wages, and parents have gone hungry in order to feed their children. So, in early March 2022, people of all ages, from all ethnic communities, came out spontaneously with home-made placards bearing slogans such as ‘Go Home Rajapaksas’. They called for democratic reform, the immediate resignation of the president and his government, and an end to economic mismanagement.

The government ignored these initial protests, but at the end of March a more militant demonstration near Gotabaya Rajapksa’s residence in Colombo was met with water cannons, tear gas and dozens of arrests.

There followed a state of emergency, plus a nationwide curfew and social media ban. This heavy-handed response was expected to stamp out the unrest, but it only enabled it to spread. The president subsequently changed tack, trying to appease the demonstrators by reversing his authoritarian measures and forcing the entire cabinet (apart from Mahinda) to resign.

But the protests kept up their momentum, and on 9 April activists occupied Galle Face Green: a park in Colombo facing the Presidential Secretariat. This now iconic site has been renamed ‘GotaGoGama’. The crowds were joined by a delegation from 1,000 different trade unions, who staged a general strike – the first in four decades – calling for the government to step down.

A month later, pro-government thugs began to carry out violent attacks on the protests. Yet their resistance was so powerful that Mahinda was ultimately compelled to step down. He was evacuated from his home by security forces while the military was deployed with shoot-on-sight orders. With international criticism of the government growing, Gotabaya installed Ranil Wickremesinghe – the leader of the United National Party – as Prime Minister on 12 May. But although Wickremesinghe may be popular with the IMF, he is deeply disliked by the masses. His proposed 21st Amendment has been widely seen as a betrayal of the protesters’ demands, and his invitation to youth groups to sit on parliamentary committees has been met with the silence it deserves.

The numbers in favour of abolishing the Executive Presidency are currently smaller than those calling for Gotabaya’s resignation, but the demand is gaining traction. This creates an opening for activists, who can now push for a broader process of political restructuring which would devolve power to provincial and local governments. It also provides a space for progressive solutions to the country’s economic crisis.

Socialist economists have long advocated a public audit that would repudiate Sri Lanka’s illegitimate debt, in defiance of the IMF. They have argued for importing only essential items like food and medicine and putting in place a public distribution system, while encouraging cooperative producers and defending public ownership of utilities, healthcare and education.

At GotaGoGama, Sinhalese and Tamils have reportedly celebrated New Year together, and various religious groups have shared in the breaking of the fast during Ramadan. Meanwhile, in the south, people have turned out for the very first time to mourn the Tamils killed in the civil war. Such developments suggest an easing of ethnic and religious tensions, despite the Rajapaksas attempts to stoke them.

When I was conducting interviews for my 1993 book, Journey Without a Destination, the vast majority of Tamil and Muslim refugees and displaced people were admirably free of ethnic hatred, despite all they had suffered. I heard numerous stories of Sinhalese friends, neighbours, colleagues and even total strangers saving the lives of Tamils.

I also encountered prejudice, especially among the Sinhalese, yet this flowed from the profound ignorance engendered by the Sinhala Only policy, as well as the suppression of dissident voices and relentless disinformation in the media. When experience contradicted propaganda, though, people were often willing to think anew. And this is precisely what is happening at present. Perhaps, if Sri Lanka’s fractured left can harness this sense of solidarity, the economic catastrophe may create the conditions for a democratic breakthrough.

si.title: පවතින ආර්ථික ව්‍යසනය, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ඛණ්ඩනය වූ වාමාංශිකයින්ට පීඩාවට පත් ජනතාව අතර මෙම නැගී එන සහයෝගීතා හැඟීම උපයෝගී කර ගත හැකි නම්, ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී ඉදිරි ගමනක් සඳහා කොන්දේසි නිර්මානය කළ හැකිය.
body_lines: මම ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ නොසිටින අතර, එහි සිදුවන දේ ගැන මට දැඩි වේදනාවක් දැනේ. මෙම අර්බුදය දශක ගණනාවක් තිස්සේ පැවති බියකරු සිහිනයක අවසානයක ආරම්භය විය හැකි බවට බලාපොරොත්තුවේ දිලිසීමකට එරෙහිව මිලියන ගණනක් ආහාර, ඉන්ධන සහ ඖෂධ හිඟයෙන් බේරෙන්නේ කෙසේද යන්න පිළිබඳ දැඩි කනස්සල්ල.

1948 දී රට නිදහස ලබා ගත් දා සිට, ජනගහනයේ විවිධ කොටස් එහි පාලක කල්ලිය විසින් ඉලක්ක කර ඇත: ඔවුන්ගේ නිවාස, ජීවනෝපායන් සහ බොහෝ විට ඔවුන්ගේ ජීවිත අහිමි වීමේ තර්ජනයට ලක්ව ඇත. ඔවුන් ආපසු සටන් කර ඇත, නමුත් වැඩි වැඩියෙන් මධ්‍යගත සහ කුරිරු රාජ්‍යයක් විසින් සෑම අංශයක්ම හුදකලා කර තලා ඇත. දැන්, පළමු වතාවට, ජනගහනයෙන් අතිමහත් බහුතරයක් කැරලි ගැසී ඇත. ආඥාදායකත්වය පිලිබඳ විවේචනය පුලුල්ව පැතිර ඇති අතර, වැඩකරන ජනතාව අතර බෙදීම් අවසානයේ සුවපත් විය හැක.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ දිගුකාලීන දේශපාලන අර්බුදයේ විවිධ කෙඳි ඉවත් කිරීම පහසු නැත, නමුත් මට උත්සාහ කිරීමට ඉඩ දෙන්න. ඕනෑම ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී ජනරජයක ප්‍රධාන අංගයක් වන නීතිය ඉදිරියේ සමානාත්මතාවයට ස්වාධීන ලංකාවේ බලය ලබා ගත් පාලක පන්තිය කිසිවිටෙකත් සහාය නොදැක්වීය. ප්‍රධාන පක්ෂ දෙක වන එක්සත් ජාතික පක්ෂය (එජාප) සහ ශ්‍රී ලංකා නිදහස් පක්ෂය (ශ්‍රීලනිප) දෙකම ‘සිංහල-බෞද්ධ’ ආධිපත්‍යවාදය අනුමත කළහ. මෙයින් අදහස් කළේ නිදහසින් පසු, මෑත කාලීන ඉන්දීය සම්භවයක් ඇති මිලියනයක් පමණ දෙමළ ජනයාගේ ඡන්ද අයිතිය අහෝසි කරමින් සහ ඔවුන්ගේ පුරවැසිභාවය ඉවත් කරමින් එක්සත් ජාතික පක්ෂය නීති සම්පාදනය කිරීමත් සමඟම ආරම්භ වූ වාර්ගික-ආගමික සුළු ජාතීන්ට එරෙහි නොනවතින වෙනස්කම් කිරීමකි. පීඩාවට පත් වූවන්ගෙන් බහුතරයක් මධ්‍යම කඳුකරයේ වතු කම්කරුවන් වූ අතර, ඔවුන් වතුකරයට කොටු වීමෙන් කම්කරු පන්තියේ අනෙකුත් කොටස් වලින් දැනටමත් හුදකලා වී ඇත.

සමානාත්මතාවයට එරෙහි මීළඟ ප්‍රධාන ප්‍රහාරය සිදු වූයේ එස්.ඩබ්ලිව්.ආර්.ඩී.ගේ නායකත්වයෙන් යුත් ශ්‍රී.ල.නි.ප. බණ්ඩාරනායක මහතා 1956 දී බලයට පැමිණ රාජ්‍ය භාෂා පනත හෙවත් ‘සිංහල පමණක් පනත් කෙටුම්පත’ සම්මත කළේය. මෙම පනත මගින් සියලුම දෙමළ කතා කරන්නන්ට, විශේෂයෙන්ම රාජ්‍ය අංශයේ රැකියා නියුක්තිකයන්ට වෙනස්කම් කර ඇත. එය 1958 දෙමළ විරෝධී කෝලාහලයෙන් පසුව විශාල විරෝධතා ඇති කළ අතර, අන්ත දක්ෂිණාංශික බෞද්ධ භික්ෂූන් ප්‍රධාන කාර්යභාරයක් ඉටු කළ අතර, දෙමළ ජනයාට හිංසා කිරීමට ප්‍රමාණවත් දුරක් නොයෑම නිසා ඊළඟ වසරේ බණ්ඩාරනායක ඝාතනය කළේය. ශ්‍රීලනිප නායකත්වය ඔහුගේ වැන්දඹු බිරිඳ වූ සිරිමාවෝ බණ්ඩාරනායක මැතිනියට හිමිවිය. ප්‍රජාවන් අතර සංවාදයට බාධා කිරීමේ හිතාමතා උත්සාහයක් ලෙස දෙමළ භාෂාව පහත හෙළන ලද අතර ඉංග්‍රීසි සම්බන්ධක භාෂාවක් ලෙස ක්‍රියා කිරීම නැවැත්වීය.

එවැනි පියවරයන්ට ට්‍රොට්ස්කිවාදී ලංකා සමසමාජ පක්ෂය (ලසසප) සහ ලංකාවේ කොමියුනිස්ට් පක්ෂය (පසුව ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ කොමියුනිස්ට් පක්ෂය, සීපීඑස්එල්) විරුද්ධ විය. එහෙත්, දෙමළ විරෝධී නීති අනුමත කිරීම වැලැක්වීමට ඔවුන් අසමත් වූ පසු, වමේ පක්ෂ විවිධ භාෂා හා ආගමික කණ්ඩායම්වල වැඩ කරන ජනතාව අතර සහයෝගීතාව ගොඩනඟමින් වෙනස්කම් කිරීමට එරෙහි අරගලය දිගටම කරගෙන ගියේ නැත.

ඒ වෙනුවට ඔවුන් 1964 දී ශ්‍රීලනිපය සමඟ සන්ධානයක් පිහිටුවා ගත් අතර, එම පක්ෂ එක්ව 1970 දී බලයට පත් වූ එක්සත් පෙරමුණ (යූඑෆ්) පිහිටුවා ගත්හ. එම අවස්ථාවේ දී වාමාංශික පක්ෂවල ප්‍රතිපත්තිගරුක සාමාජිකයන් බෙදී ගිය අතර දෙමළ සමාජවාදීන් අධෛර්යයට පත් විය. ඔවුන්ගේ නායකයන්ගේ යටත් වීමෙනි. ප‍්‍රජාතන්ත‍්‍රවාදී විප්ලවය හරහා ගෙන යා හැකිව තිබූ එකම බලවේගය දෙකඩ වී තිබුණි.

බලයට පත් වූ පසු, යූඑෆ් හි 1972 සහ 1975 ඉඩම් ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ නීති මගින් වතු ජනසතු කරන ලදී. එහෙත් එලවා දමා බඩගින්නේ සිටින දෙමළ කම්කරුවන්ට ඉඩම් බෙදා දෙනවාට වඩා එය සිංහල ආණ්ඩුවේ ආධාරකරුවන්ට ලබා දෙන ලදී. ඊට ප්‍රතිචාර වශයෙන් දෙමළ එක්සත් විමුක්ති පෙරමුණ (TULF) ස්වාධීන දෙමළ ඊළමක් නිර්මාණය කරන ලෙස ඉල්ලා සිටියේය. මෙම ඉලක්කය සඳහා සටන් කිරීම සඳහා සටන්කාමී කණ්ඩායම්, විශේෂයෙන්ම දෙමළ ඊළාම් විමුක්ති කොටි (එල්ටීටීඊ) පිහිටුවන ලදී.

එල්ටීටීඊය ස්වයං නිර්ණය සඳහා යුක්තිසහගත අරගලයක් දියත් කරන බව විශ්වාස කළ සමහර දෙමළ සමාජවාදීන්ගේ ආකර්ශනය ආරම්භ විය. එහෙත්, යථාර්ථයේ දී, මෙම කණ්ඩායම සෑම විටම සිංහලයන් වාර්ගික වශයෙන් ශුද්ධ කර මරා දමමින් දෙමළ-උත්තමාධිපත්‍යවාදී රාජ්‍යයක් නිර්මාණය කිරීමට කැපවී සිටියහ. එය තම භූමිය යැයි කියාගත් උතුරු හා නැඟෙනහිර පළාත්වල දෙමළ කතා කරන මුස්ලිම් ජනතාව පවා ඉලක්ක කළේය.

යූඑෆ් විසින් සිංහල බහුතරයට ලබා දුන් සියලු වරප්‍රසාද නොතකා, පාලන තන්ත්‍රය පිළිබඳ අතෘප්තිය පුළුල් ලෙස පැවතුනි. උද්ධමනය සහ ආහාර හිඟය හේතුවෙන් සෞඛ්‍ය හා අධ්‍යාපනයේ සැලකිය යුතු ජයග්‍රහණ මහජන මනසින් අවලංගු විය. 1971 දී රෝහණ විජේවීරගේ නායකත්වයෙන් යුත් ජනතා විමුක්ති පෙරමුණ (ජවිපෙ හෝ මහජන විමුක්ති පෙරමුණ) රජය පෙරළා දැමීම සඳහා සන්නද්ධ නැගිටීමක් දියත් කළේය - විරැකියාව සහ දරිද්‍රතාවයේ ප්‍රශ්න සිංහලෙන් පමණක් විසඳා නොතිබූ සිංහල තරුණයින්ගේ අතිමහත් සහයෝගය ඇත. .

‘නූතන බොල්ෂෙවික්’ ලෙස ඔහුගේ ස්වයං විස්තරය නොතකා, දෙමළ වතු කම්කරුවන් ඉන්දියානු ව්‍යාප්තවාදයේ හුදු මෙවලම් ලෙස හුවා දක්වන සිංහල අධිපතිවාදී දෘෂ්ටියකින් විජේවීරගේ විප්ලවවාදී ක්ෂිතිජය පටු විය. ඔහුගේ නැගිටීම වාර්ගික රේඛා හරහා ළඟා වීමට බල රහිත වූ අතර හදිසි තත්වයක් ප්‍රකාශයට පත් කිරීමෙන් පසු අවසානයේ UF රජය විසින් තලා දැමීය. එසේ වුවද, ආණ්ඩු පක්ෂය කෙරෙහි ද්වේෂය පවතියි. 1977 පාර්ලිමේන්තු මැතිවරණය පැවැත්වෙද්දී, ජේ.ආර්. ජයවර්ධනගේ නායකත්වයෙන් යුත් එජාපය ආසන 168 න් 140 ක් ලබාගෙන නැවත බලයට පත් විය. ඔහු නව ව්‍යවස්ථාවක් සම්මත කර ගැනීමට සහ අසීමිත බලතලවලින් විධායක ජනාධිපති ලෙස අභිෂේක කිරීමට මෙම සුපිරි බහුතරය භාවිතා කළේය.

ජයවර්ධන යටතේ සිංහල ආධිපත්‍යය තහවුරු විය, ඔහු තේරී පත්වී මාසයකට පසු දෙමල විරෝධී සංහාරයන් රට පුරා පැතිර ගියේය. 1979 ත්‍රස්තවාදය වැලැක්වීමේ පනත මගින් දෙමළ ජනයා දහස් ගණනකට වධහිංසා පැමිණවීම, අතුරුදහන් කිරීම සහ නීති විරෝධී ලෙස ඝාතනය කිරීම වේගවත් විය. 1981 දී, යාපනයේ රාජ්‍ය අනුග්‍රහය ඇතිව ගිනි තැබීම්, ස්ත්‍රී දූෂණය සහ කොල්ලකෑම්වල විනෝදාස්වාදයක් වූයේ මහජන පුස්තකාලය ගිනි තැබීම, පොත් සහ පුරාණ අත්පිටපත් 95,000 ක් පමණ ඇතුලේය. පසුව ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය නැගෙනහිර, දකුණ සහ කඳුකර ප්‍රදේශවලට මාරු වූ අතර දෙමළ ජනයා දහස් ගණනක් ඔවුන්ගේ නිවෙස්වලින් ඉවත් කර ඔවුන්ගේ දේපළ කොල්ලකෑහ. 1983 ඊටත් වඩා බිහිසුණු සමූලඝාතන එල්ටීටීඊය සහ රජය අතර සිවිල් යුද්ධයක් ආරම්භ කළ අතර එය වසර 26ක් පමණ පැවතුනි.

මේ සියල්ල ජයවර්ධනගේ පූර්වගාමීන් විසින් ඇති කරන ලද ප්‍රවණතාවය ස්ථිර කළේය. එහෙත් ඔහුගේ පාලන තන්ත්‍රය කැපී පෙනුනේ එහි විනාශකාරී නව ලිබරල්කරණ වැඩසටහන සහ නිර්ලජ්ජිත අධිකාරීවාදයයි. කෘෂිකාර්මික සහ කාර්මික යන දෙඅංශයේම පාරිභෝගික භාණ්ඩ නිෂ්පාදනය 1980 ගණන්වල ලාභදායී ආනයනයන් නිසා පීඩාවට පත් වූ අතර, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ කලින් නොතිබූ සුඛෝපභෝගී භාණ්ඩ ආනයනය කිරීම විදේශ විනිමය කාණුවට එකතු විය. සංක්‍රමණික සේවකයන්ගේ ප්‍රේෂණ, තේ අපනයනය, සංචාරක ව්‍යාපාරය සහ නව විදේශ ආයෝජන, ත්‍යාගශීලී බදු නිවාඩු සහ තීරුබදු රහිත යෙදවුම් ආනයනය හේතුවෙන් හිඩැස පිරවීමට අසමත් විය. මෙය වත්මන් ආර්ථික අර්බුදයට පදනම දමමින් විදේශ ණය මත යැපීම වැඩි කිරීමට හේතු විය.

මේ අතර, ජයවර්ධන සියලු විසම්මුතීන් තලා දැමීමට සහ ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදය නිවා දැමීමට උත්සාහ කළේය. ඔහුගේ අලුතින් පිහිටුවන ලද ජාතික සේවක සංගමය (ජේඑස්එස්) දෙමල විරෝධී සංහාරයන් මෙන්ම, විරුද්ධ පාක්ෂිකයන්, විනිසුරුවන්, වෘත්තීය සමිතිකරුවන් සහ වැඩ වර්ජන කරන කම්කරුවන් ඝාතනය කිරීම සඳහා යොදවන ලදී. 1987 මැද භාගයේ ආරම්භ වී 1989 අග භාගයේ විෙජ්වීර අල්ලා මරාදැමීමත් සමඟ අවසන් වූ ජවිපෙ දෙවන කැරැල්ල, එම කාලය තුළ පමණක් සිංහලයන් 40,000-60,000ක් පමණ ඝාතනය කර ඇත. ජයවර්ධනගේ සහ ඔහුගේ අනුප්‍රාප්තිකයා වූ රණසිංහ ප්‍රේමදාසගේ විරුද්ධවාදීන් ඉලක්ක කරගත් ඝාතක කල්ලි නිතර ඔවුන්ව පැහැරගෙන ගොස් වධ දී මරා දැමීය. වත්මන් ශ්‍රී ලංකා අග්‍රාමාත්‍ය රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ මහතා මේ කාලය පුරාවටම රජයේ අමාත්‍යවරයෙකි. වත්මන් ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ හමුදාපතිවරයෙකි. දෙමල සහ සිංහල සමූහ ඝාතනයට දෙදෙනාම සම්බන්ධ විය.

එදා සිටම විධායක ජනාධිපති ක්‍රමය දිගින් දිගටම කඹ ඇදිල්ලකට ලක් විය. මේ සඳහා පාර්ලිමේන්තුවේ තුනෙන් දෙකක බහුතරයක් සහ ජනමත විචාරණයකින් නිරපේක්ෂ බහුතරයක් අවශ්‍ය බව අධිකරණය විසින් තීන්දු කර ඇති නිසා එය අහෝසි කිරීම මේ වන විට නොපැහැදිලි බව ඔප්පු වී ඇත. එහෙත් ජනාධිපති ධුරයේ බලය සීමා කිරීම සඳහා වඩාත් නිහතමානී ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ ඉඳහිට සම්මත කර ඇත. 1994 සිට 2005 දක්වා චන්ද්‍රිකා කුමාරතුංග මහත්මියගේ ජනාධිපති ධූරය යටතේ ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදයට එල්ල වූ ප්‍රහාර අඩු වූ අතර මැතිවරණ කොමිසම, ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය වැනි ආයතනවලට ඒකපාර්ශ්විකව පුද්ගලයන් පත් කිරීමට ජනාධිපතිවරයාට තිබූ හැකියාව ඉවත් කරමින් 17 වැනි සංශෝධනය ගෙන එන ලදී. මෙම තාවකාලික ප්‍රගතිය මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ යටතේ ආපසු හැරවිය, මන්ද රාජ්‍ය අනුග්‍රහය ලබන ඝාතක කණ්ඩායම් විරුද්ධවාදීන් ඉලක්ක කර ගැනීම සඳහා යලි පණ ගැන්වූ බැවිනි. 2009 දී, 40,000 දෙමළ සිවිල් වැසියන් ඝාතනය කරන ලද සිවිල් යුද්ධයේ බිහිසුණු උච්චතම අවස්ථාවේ දී එල්ටීටීඊය අවසානයේ පරාජය කරන ලදී. ඊට සමගාමීව, 18 වැනි ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා සංශෝධනයක් කුමාරතුංගගේ ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණ ආපසු හැරවූ අතර ජනාධිපති ධුරයේ වාර දෙකක සීමාව අහෝසි කළේය.

මෙය ‘අධිරාජ්‍ය විරෝධී’ ආස්ථානයක් ලෙස හුවා දක්වමින් යුද අපරාධ පිළිබඳ වාර්තා සම්බන්ධයෙන් ස්වාධීන පරීක්ෂණයක් සඳහා එක්සත් ජාතීන්ගේ ඉල්ලීම රාජපක්ෂ ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළේය. එහෙත් ඔහුගේ වාචාලකම සමහර ඡන්දදායකයින් සමඟ හොඳින් ක්‍රීඩා කළද, රටේ විදේශ ණය කන්දට - චීන රජය ඇතුළු නව ණය හිමියන් සහ ස්වෛරී බැඳුම්කර පෞද්ගලික ගැනුම්කරුවන් සමඟ දායක වීමෙන් ඔහුට විශ්වාසනීයත්වය නැති විය. ඔවුන්ගේ ජනප්‍රියත්වය පිරිහීමත් සමග, ජනාධිපතිවරයා සහ ඔහුගේ සොහොයුරු ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ - ආරක්ෂක ලේකම්වරයා ලෙස, බුද්ධි අංශ පාලනය කළ - මුස්ලිම් ජනතාව බිල්ලට දීමෙන් ඔවුන්ගේ වෘත්තීය ජීවිතය බේරා ගැනීමට උත්සාහ කළහ.

රාජ්‍ය පාලිත මාධ්‍ය හරහා ඉස්ලාම් භීතිකාව ප්‍රචාරක ව්‍යාපාරයක් දියත් කරන අතරතුර, ඔවුන් මුස්ලිම්වරුන්ට ප්‍රචණ්ඩ ලෙස පහර දීමට යොදා ගනිමින්, අන්ත දක්ෂිනාංශික බෞද්ධ භික්ෂූන් කන්ඩායම්වලට අරමුදල් සැපයූහ. රේඩාර් යටතේ, රාජපක්ෂලා එල්ටීටීඊයට එරෙහිව සටන් කිරීම සඳහා ඉස්ලාමීය සටන්කාමීන්ට ද අරමුදල් සැපයූහ - ඔවුන් රැඩිකල්කරණයට ලක්ව ඇති බවට විශ්වාසදායක බුද්ධි තොරතුරු නොතකා ඔත්තුකරුවන් ලෙස රජයේ වැටුප් ලේඛනයේ රැඳී සිටියහ.

මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ 2015 දී බලයෙන් පහ කරනු ලැබුවේ, ඔහුගේ පාලනයේ අපකීර්තිමත් ඥාති සංග්‍රහය සහ දූෂණය සමඟින් සිංහල ජනතාවගෙන් විශාල කොටසක් ඈත් කරමිනි. ඔහු වෙනුවට වාර්ගික සුළුතරයන් ශ්‍රීලනිප කැරලිකාර මෛත්‍රීපාල සිරිසේන සහ රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ අතර බිඳෙනසුලු යහපාලන (යහපාලනය) සභාගයකට ඡන්දය දුන්හ.

නව සභාගය නැවත වරක් ජනාධිපතිවරයාගේ බලතල කප්පාදු කළ අතර 19 වැනි සංශෝධනයෙන් වාර දෙකක සීමාව නැවත ස්ථාපිත කළේය. නමුත් එයද කඩා වැටුණි. එහි විශ්වසනීයත්වයට එල්ල වූ අවසාන පහර වූයේ 2019 පාස්කු ඉරිදා ත්‍රස්ත ප්‍රහාරය වන අතර එමඟින් රට පුරා ස්ථානවල පුද්ගලයින් 269 දෙනෙකු මිය ගියහ. එයින් පෙනී ගිය පරිදි, බෝම්බ ප්‍රහාර එල්ල කර ඇත්තේ රාජපක්ෂලා බැංකු ගත කළ ඉස්ලාම්වාදීන්මය. යහපාලන රජය සමයේ මහ මොළකරු සහරාන් හෂීම් ඇතුළු ත්‍රස්තවාදී කණ්ඩායමේ සාමාජිකයින්ට ගෝඨාභයට පක්ෂපාතීව කටයුතු කළ නිලධාරීන් විසින් දිගින් දිගටම මුදල් ගෙවා නඩු පැවරීමෙන් ආරක්ෂා කර ඇති බව පසුව සිදුකළ විමර්ශනවලදී අනාවරණය විය. මෙය සිදු වූයේ හෂීම් ISIS වෙත පක්ෂපාතී බව ප්‍රකාශ කර තිබියදීත්, ඔහුගේ අනුගාමිකයින් ආයුධ සහ පුපුරණ ද්‍රව්‍ය ගොඩගසා ගන්නා බවට ඕනෑ තරම් සාක්ෂි තිබියදීත් ය.

එහෙත් ඊට පටහැනිව, පාස්කු ඉරිදා සංහාරයේ වාසිය ලැබුවේ රාජපක්ෂලාට ය. එහි ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස ඇති වූ භීතිය තුළ ආන්ඩුව දුර්වල වූ අතර 'ජාතික ආරක්ෂාව' අපේක්ෂකයා ලෙස ඉදිරිපත් වෙමින් ඵලදායී ජනාධිපතිවරණ ව්‍යාපාරයක් දියත් කිරීමට ගෝඨාභයට හැකි විය. එම වසරේ අගභාගයේදී ඔහු 52% ක ඡන්ද ප්‍රතිශතයක් ලබා ගනිමින් මත විමසුමේ ඉහළින්ම සිටියේය. රාජපක්ෂලාගේ නව පක්ෂය වන ශ්‍රී ලංකා පොදුජන පෙරමුණේ නායකත්වයෙන් යුත් සන්ධානයක් ද 19 වැනි සංශෝධනය ආපස්සට කරමින් 20 වැනි සංශෝධනය සම්මත කිරීමට තරම් විශාල බහුතරයකින් පාර්ලිමේන්තු මැතිවරණය ජයග්‍රහණය කළේය. ගෝඨාභය අගමැති ධූරයට පත් වූ මහින්ද ඇතුළු පවුලේ සාමාජිකයන් සමඟ කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලය අසුරන්නට විය.

මේ කාලය වන විට ආර්ථිකය ඩොලර් බිලියන 51 ක විදේශ නය යටතේ ගිලා බැස ඇති අතර, එයින් වැඩි කොටසක් පවුලේ නිෂ්ඵල ව්‍යාපෘති සහ රටින් පිටට නිමක් නැති මුදල් ඉවත දැමීමෙන් සිදු විය. ගෝඨාභය රෙජීමයේ බදු අඩුකිරීම් ණය දරාගත නොහැකි තත්ත්වයට පත් කළ අතර, ගොවි විරෝධතා හමුවේ ක්‍රියාත්මක වූ රසායනික පොහොර ආනයනය එක රැයකින් තහනම් කිරීම - බෝග අස්වැන්නේ දැවැන්ත පහත වැටීමකට හේතු විය. විදේශ සංචිත අවසන් වූ විට, දේශීය ආහාර නිෂ්පාදනය සහ අපනයන පහත වැටුණු අතර, විරැකියාව, අහස උසට නැඟෙන උද්ධමනය, විදුලි කප්පාදුව සහ මූලික භාණ්ඩ මිලදී ගැනීම සඳහා දිගු පෝලිම් ඇති විය.

ඉතා ධනවතුන් හැර අන් සියල්ලෝම මෙම දියවීම නිසා පීඩාවට පත්ව සිටිති. කම්කරුවන්ට රැකියා අහිමි වී ගොවීන් අර්බුදයට ලක්ව ඇති අතර ධීවරයින්ට තම බෝට්ටු බල ගැන්වීමට ඉන්ධන නොමැත. වේගයෙන් ඉහළ යන උද්ධමනය වැටුප් ඛාදනය වී ඇති අතර, තම දරුවන්ට පෝෂණය කිරීම සඳහා දෙමාපියන් කුසගින්නේ සිටිති. ඉතින්, 2022 මාර්තු මස මුලදී, සෑම ජනවාර්ගික ප්‍රජාවකටම අයත් සෑම වයස් කාණ්ඩයකම මිනිසුන්, ‘රාජපක්ෂලා ගෙදර යන්න’ වැනි සටන් පාඨ රැගත් ගෙදර හැදූ පුවරු රැගෙන නිරායාසයෙන් එළියට ආහ. ප‍්‍රජාතන්ත‍්‍රවාදී ප‍්‍රතිසංස්කරණ, ජනාධිපතිවරයා සහ ඔහුගේ ආන්ඩුව වහා ඉල්ලා අස්විය යුතු බවත්, ආර්ථික අවභාවිතය අවසන් කරන ලෙසත් ඔවුහු ඉල්ලා සිටියහ.
ආන්ඩුව මෙම මූලික විරෝධතා නොතකා හැරියේය, නමුත් මාර්තු අවසානයේ කොළඹ ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂගේ නිවස අසල වඩාත් සටන්කාමී උද්ඝෝෂණයක් ජල ප්‍රහාර, කඳුළු ගෑස් සහ අත්අඩංගුවට ගැනීම් දුසිම් ගනනක් සමඟ මුණගැසුණි.

හදිසි තත්වයක්, ඊට අමතරව රටපුරා ඇඳිරි නීතිය සහ සමාජ මාධ්‍ය තහනමක් අනුගමනය කරන ලදී. මෙම දැඩි ප්‍රතිචාරය නොසන්සුන්තාව මැඩලීමට අපේක්ෂා කළ නමුත් එය එය පැතිරීමට පමණක් හැකි විය. පසුව ජනාධිපතිවරයා තම අත්තනෝමතික ක්‍රියාමාර්ග ආපසු හරවා ගනිමින් සහ සමස්ත කැබිනට් මණ්ඩලයටම (මහින්ද හැර) ඉල්ලා අස්වීමට බල කරමින් පෙලපාලිකරුවන් සනසන්නට උත්සාහ කරමින් උපාය වෙනස් කළේය.

එහෙත් විරෝධතා ඔවුන්ගේ වේගවත්ව පැවති අතර අප්‍රේල් 9 වැනි දින ක්‍රියාකාරීන් ජනාධිපති ලේකම් කාර්යාලයට මුහුණලා කොළඹ පිහිටි ගාලු මුවදොර පිටිය අල්ලා ගත්හ. මෙම දැන් සංකේතාත්මක වෙබ් අඩවිය 'GotaGoGama' ලෙස නම් කර ඇත. විවිධ වෘත්තීය සමිති 1,000 ක නියෝජිත පිරිසක් ද ජනකායට එක් වූ අතර, ඔවුන් මහා වැඩ වර්ජනයක් - දශක හතරකට පසු පළමු වතාවට - ආන්ඩුව ඉවත් වන ලෙස ඉල්ලා සිටියහ.

මාසයකට පසු, රජයට පක්ෂපාතී මැරයන් විරෝධතාවලට ප්‍රචණ්ඩ ප්‍රහාර එල්ල කිරීමට පටන් ගත්හ. එහෙත් ඔවුන්ගේ ප්‍රතිරෝධය කෙතරම් ප්‍රබලද යත් අවසානයේ මහින්දට බලයෙන් ඉවත් වීමට සිදු විය. බැලූ බැල්මට වෙඩි තැබීමේ නියෝග සහිතව හමුදාව යොදවා තිබියදී ආරක්ෂක අංශ විසින් ඔහුව ඔහුගේ නිවසින් ඉවත් කරන ලදී. ආණ්ඩුවට එරෙහිව ජාත්‍යන්තර විවේචන වර්ධනය වීමත් සමඟ ගෝඨාභය විසින් මැයි 12 වැනිදා එක්සත් ජාතික පක්ෂයේ නායක රනිල් වික්‍රමසිංහ අගමැති ලෙස පත් කළේය. එහෙත්, වික්‍රමසිංහ ජාත්‍යන්තර මූල්‍ය අරමුදලේ ජනප්‍රිය විය හැකි වුවද, ඔහු මහජනතාවගේ දැඩි අප්‍රසාදයට ලක්ව ඇත. ඔහුගේ යෝජිත 21 වන සංශෝධනය විරෝධතාකරුවන්ගේ ඉල්ලීම් පාවා දීමක් ලෙස පුළුල් ලෙස සලකනු ලැබ ඇති අතර, තරුණ කණ්ඩායම්වලට පාර්ලිමේන්තු කමිටුවලට අසුන් ගන්නා ලෙස ඔහුගේ ආරාධනය ලැබිය යුතු නිශ්ශබ්දතාවයකින් යුක්ත වේ.

විධායක ජනාධිපති ක‍්‍රමය අහෝසි කිරීමට පක්ෂව සිටින සංඛ්‍යාව දැනට ගෝඨාභයගේ ඉල්ලා අස්විය යුතු යැයි කියන සංඛ්‍යාවට වඩා කුඩා නමුත් ඉල්ලුම වැඩි වෙමින් පවතී. මෙය ක්‍රියාකාරීන් සඳහා විවරයක් නිර්මාණය කරයි, ඔවුන්ට දැන් පළාත් සහ පළාත් පාලනවලට බලය බෙදාහරින දේශපාලන ප්‍රතිව්‍යුහගත කිරීමේ පුළුල් ක්‍රියාවලියක් සඳහා තල්ලු කළ හැකිය. එය රටේ ආර්ථික අර්බුදයට ප්‍රගතිශීලී විසඳුම් සඳහා අවකාශයක් ද සපයයි.

සමාජවාදී අර්ථශාස්ත්‍රඥයින් ජාමූඅ නොතකා ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ නීත්‍යානුකූල නොවන නය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කරන මහජන විගණනයක් දිගු කලක් තිස්සේ යෝජනා කර ඇත. සමුපකාර නිෂ්පාදකයින් දිරිමත් කරමින් උපයෝගිතා, සෞඛ්‍ය සහ අධ්‍යාපනයේ පොදු අයිතිය ආරක්ෂා කරන අතරම ආහාර සහ ඖෂධ වැනි අත්‍යාවශ්‍ය ද්‍රව්‍ය පමණක් ආනයනය කර පොදු බෙදා හැරීමේ ක්‍රමයක් ක්‍රියාත්මක කිරීමට ඔවුන් තර්ක කර ඇත.

GotaGoGama හි සිංහල සහ දෙමළ ජනතාව එක්ව අලුත් අවුරුද්ද සැමරූ බව වාර්තා වන අතර රාමසාන් සමයේ උපවාසය අත්හැරීමට විවිධ ආගමික කණ්ඩායම් හවුල් වී ඇත. මේ අතර, සිවිල් යුද්ධයෙන් මියගිය දෙමළ ජනතාව වෙනුවෙන් ශෝකය පළ කිරීමට දකුණේ ජනතාව පළමු වරට පැමිණ තිබේ. රාජපක්ෂලා විසින් ජනවාර්ගික හා ආගමික ආතතීන් අවුලුවාලීමට උත්සාහ කළද, එවැනි වර්ධනයන් යෝජනා කරන්නේ ඒවා ලිහිල් කිරීමක් ය.

මම 1993 මගේ ගමනාන්තයක් නැති ගමනක් පොත සඳහා සම්මුඛ පරීක්ෂණ පවත්වමින් සිටියදී, දෙමළ හා මුස්ලිම් සරණාගතයින් සහ අවතැන් වූවන්ගෙන් අතිමහත් බහුතරයක් වාර්ගික වෛරයකින් තොරව ප්‍රශංසනීය ලෙස දුක් වින්දා. සිංහල මිතුරන්, අසල්වාසීන්, සගයන් සහ සම්පූර්ණයෙන්ම නාඳුනන පුද්ගලයින් පවා දෙමළ ජනයාගේ ජීවිත බේරාගැනීමේ කථා මා අසා ඇත.

විශේෂයෙන්ම සිංහලයන් අතර අගතියක් ද මට ඇති විය, නමුත් මෙය ගලා ආවේ සිංහල පමණක් ප්‍රතිපත්තිය විසින් ජනිත කරන ලද ගැඹුරු නොදැනුවත්කම මෙන්ම විසම්මුතික හඬවල් මර්දනය කිරීම සහ මාධ්‍ය තුළ නිර්දය අසත්‍ය තොරතුරු යටපත් කිරීමෙනි. කෙසේවෙතත්, අත්දැකීම් ප්‍රචාරණයට පටහැනි වූ විට, මිනිසුන් බොහෝ විට අලුතින් සිතීමට කැමති විය. එමෙන්ම වර්තමානයේ සිදුවෙමින් පවතින්නේ මෙයයි. සමහර විට, ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ කැඩුණු වමට මෙම සහයෝගීතාවයේ හැඟීම උපයෝගී කර ගත හැකි නම්, ආර්ථික ව්‍යසනය ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදී ඉදිරි ගමනක් සඳහා කොන්දේසි නිර්මානය කරනු ඇත.

ta.title: இலங்கையின் பிளவுபட்ட இடதுசாரிகள் பாதிக்கப்பட்ட மக்களிடையே உருவாகி வரும் இந்த ஒற்றுமை உணர்வைப் பயன்படுத்தினால், நிலவும் பொருளாதாரப் பேரழிவு ஜனநாயக முன்னேற்றத்திற்கான சூழ்நிலையை உருவாக்கலாம்.
body_lines: நான் இலங்கையில் இல்லை, அங்கு என்ன நடக்கிறது என்பதைப் பற்றி நான் வேதனைப்படுகிறேன். உணவு, எரிபொருள் மற்றும் மருந்துப் பற்றாக்குறையிலிருந்து மில்லியன் கணக்கான மக்கள் எப்படித் தப்பிப்பிழைப்பார்கள் என்பது பற்றிய கடுமையான கவலை, இந்த நெருக்கடியானது பல தசாப்தங்களாக நீடித்த கனவின் முடிவின் தொடக்கமாக இருக்கும் என்ற நம்பிக்கையின் ஒரு பிரகாசத்திற்கு எதிராக.

1948 இல் நாடு சுதந்திரம் பெற்றதில் இருந்து, மக்கள்தொகையின் பல்வேறு பிரிவுகள் அதன் ஆளும் கும்பலால் குறிவைக்கப்பட்டுள்ளனர்: அவர்களின் வீடுகள், வாழ்வாதாரங்கள் மற்றும் பெரும்பாலும் தங்கள் உயிர்களை இழக்கும் அச்சுறுத்தலுக்கு ஆளாகியுள்ளனர். அவர்கள் மீண்டும் போராடினர், ஆனால் ஒவ்வொரு பிரிவும் பெருகிய முறையில் மையப்படுத்தப்பட்ட மற்றும் இரக்கமற்ற அரசால் தனிமைப்படுத்தப்பட்டு நசுக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. இப்போது, ​​முதன்முறையாக, பெரும்பான்மையான மக்கள் கிளர்ச்சியில் எழுந்துள்ளனர். சர்வாதிகாரத்தின் மீதான விமர்சனம் பரவலாக உள்ளது, மேலும் உழைக்கும் மக்களிடையே பிளவுகள் இறுதியாக குணமாகும்.

இலங்கையின் நீண்டகால அரசியல் நெருக்கடியின் பல்வேறு இழைகளைப் பிரிப்பது எளிதல்ல, ஆனால் நான் முயற்சிக்கிறேன். சட்டத்தின் முன் சமத்துவம் - எந்தவொரு ஜனநாயகக் குடியரசின் முக்கிய கூறுபாடு - சுதந்திர இலங்கையில் அதிகாரத்தைக் கைப்பற்றிய ஆளும் வர்க்கத்தால் ஒருபோதும் ஆதரிக்கப்படவில்லை. ஐக்கிய தேசியக் கட்சி (UNP) மற்றும் ஸ்ரீலங்கா சுதந்திரக் கட்சி (SLFP) ஆகிய இரண்டு பிரதான கட்சிகளும் ‘சிங்கள-பௌத்த’ மேலாதிக்கத்தை அங்கீகரித்தன. இது இன-மத சிறுபான்மையினருக்கு எதிரான தொடர்ச்சியான பாகுபாட்டைக் குறிக்கிறது, இது சுதந்திரத்திற்குப் பிறகு தொடங்கியது, UNP சமீபத்திய இந்திய வம்சாவளியைச் சேர்ந்த ஒரு மில்லியன் தமிழர்களின் வாக்குரிமையை மறுத்து அவர்களின் குடியுரிமையைப் பறிக்கும் சட்டத்தை இயற்றியது. பாதிக்கப்பட்டவர்களில் பெரும்பாலோர் மத்திய மலைநாட்டில் உள்ள தோட்டத் தொழிலாளர்கள், அவர்கள் ஏற்கனவே தோட்டங்களில் அடைத்துவைக்கப்பட்டதன் மூலம் தொழிலாள வர்க்கத்தின் மற்ற பிரிவுகளில் இருந்து தனிமைப்படுத்தப்பட்டிருந்தனர்.

S.W.R.D தலைமையிலான SLFP ஆனது சமத்துவத்தின் மீதான அடுத்த பெரிய தாக்குதல். பண்டாரநாயக்கா 1956 இல் ஆட்சிக்கு வந்து அரச மொழிச் சட்டம் அல்லது ‘சிங்களம் மட்டும் சட்டமூலம்’ இயற்றினார். இச்சட்டம் அனைத்து தமிழ் பேசும் மக்களுக்கும், குறிப்பாக பொதுத்துறை வேலைவாய்ப்பில் பாரபட்சம் காட்டியது. இது 1958 ஆம் ஆண்டு தமிழர்-விரோதக் கலவரத்தைத் தொடர்ந்து பெரும் எதிர்ப்புக்களைத் தூண்டியது, இதில் தீவிர வலதுசாரி பௌத்த பிக்குகள் பெரும் பங்கு வகித்தனர், அடுத்த ஆண்டு தமிழர்களைத் துன்புறுத்துவதில் போதுமான அளவு செல்லாததற்காக பண்டாரநாயக்காவை படுகொலை செய்தனர். ஸ்ரீலங்கா சுதந்திரக் கட்சியின் தலைமைத்துவத்தை அவரது விதவையான சிறிமாவோ பண்டாரநாயக்கா கைப்பற்றினார். சமூகங்களுக்கிடையேயான உரையாடலைத் தடுக்கும் முயற்சியில், தமிழ் தரமிறக்கப்பட்டது மற்றும் ஆங்கிலம் இணைப்பு மொழியாக செயல்படுவதை நிறுத்தியது.

இத்தகைய நடவடிக்கைகளை ட்ரொட்ஸ்கிச லங்கா சமசமாஜ கட்சி (LSSP) மற்றும் இலங்கை கம்யூனிஸ்ட் கட்சி (பின்னர் இலங்கை கம்யூனிஸ்ட் கட்சி, CPSL) எதிர்த்தன. ஆயினும்கூட, தமிழர்களுக்கு எதிரான சட்டங்கள் அங்கீகரிக்கப்படுவதைத் தடுக்கத் தவறியவுடன், இடதுசாரிக் கட்சிகள் பல்வேறு மொழி மற்றும் மத குழுக்களைச் சேர்ந்த உழைக்கும் மக்களிடையே ஒற்றுமையைக் கட்டியெழுப்புவதன் மூலம் பாகுபாட்டிற்கு எதிரான போராட்டத்தைத் தொடரவில்லை.

மாறாக, அவர்கள் 1964 இல் ஸ்ரீலங்கா சுதந்திரக் கட்சியுடன் ஒரு கூட்டணியை உருவாக்கினர், மேலும் கட்சிகள் கூட்டாக ஐக்கிய முன்னணியை (UF) ஸ்தாபித்தன, அது 1970 இல் ஆட்சியைப் பிடித்தது. அந்த நேரத்தில், இடது கட்சிகளின் கொள்கை ரீதியான உறுப்பினர்கள் பிரிந்து, தமிழ் சோசலிஸ்டுகள் மனச்சோர்வடைந்தனர். அவர்களின் தலைவர்களின் சரணாகதியால். ஜனநாயகப் புரட்சியின் மூலம் கொண்டு செல்லக்கூடிய ஒரே சக்தி பிளவுபட்டது.

ஆட்சிக்கு வந்ததும், 1972 மற்றும் 1975 இன் UF இன் நிலச் சீர்திருத்தச் சட்டங்கள் தோட்டங்களை தேசியமயமாக்கின. இருந்தும் தமிழ் தொழிலாளர்களுக்கு காணிகளை பகிர்ந்தளிப்பதற்கு பதிலாக - வெளியேற்றப்பட்டு பட்டினி கிடக்கும் - சிங்கள அரசாங்க ஆதரவாளர்களிடம் ஒப்படைக்கப்பட்டது. இதற்குப் பதிலளித்த தமிழர் ஐக்கிய விடுதலை முன்னணி (TULF) சுதந்திர தமிழீழம் உருவாக்கப்பட வேண்டும் என்று கூறியது. போராளிக் குழுக்கள், குறிப்பாக தமிழீழ விடுதலைப் புலிகள் (LTTE), இந்த இலக்குக்காகப் போராட ஸ்தாபிக்கப்பட்டது.

எல்.ரீ.ரீ.ஈ ஆரம்பத்தில் சில தமிழ் சோசலிஸ்டுகளை ஈர்த்தது, அவர்கள் சுயநிர்ணய உரிமைக்கான நியாயமான போராட்டத்தை நடத்துவதாக நம்பினர்; ஆனால், உண்மையில், சிங்களவர்களை இனச் சுத்திகரிப்பு மற்றும் கொலை மூலம் தமிழ் மேலாதிக்க அரசை உருவாக்குவதில் குழு எப்போதும் உறுதிபூண்டிருந்தது. அது வடக்கு மற்றும் கிழக்கு மாகாணங்களில் உள்ள தமிழ் பேசும் முஸ்லீம்களைக் கூட குறிவைத்தது.

சிங்கள பெரும்பான்மையினருக்கு UF வழங்கிய அனைத்து சலுகைகளும் இருந்தபோதிலும், ஆட்சியின் மீதான அதிருப்தி பரவலாக இருந்தது. பணவீக்கம் மற்றும் உணவுப் பற்றாக்குறையால் சுகாதாரம் மற்றும் கல்வியில் கணிசமான லாபங்கள் மக்கள் மனதில் ரத்து செய்யப்பட்டன. 1971 இல், ரோஹன விஜேவீர தலைமையிலான ஜனதா விமுக்தி பெரமுனா (ஜே.வி.பி அல்லது மக்கள் விடுதலை முன்னணி), அரசாங்கத்தை கவிழ்க்க ஆயுதமேந்திய எழுச்சியை நடத்தியது - வேலையின்மை மற்றும் வறுமை போன்ற பிரச்சினைகளுக்கு சிங்களத்தால் மட்டுமே தீர்வு காணப்படாத சிங்கள இளைஞர்களின் பெரும் ஆதரவுடன். .

ஒரு 'நவீன போல்ஷிவிக்' என்று அவர் சுயவிளக்கம் செய்த போதிலும், விஜேவீரவின் புரட்சிகர எல்லைகள் ஒரு சிங்கள மேலாதிக்கக் கண்ணோட்டத்தால் சுருக்கப்பட்டது, இது தமிழ் தோட்டத் தொழிலாளர்களை இந்திய விரிவாக்கவாதத்தின் கருவிகளாகக் காட்டியது. அவரது எழுச்சியானது இன எல்லைகளைக் கடந்து செல்வதற்கு சக்தியற்றதாக இருந்தது, இறுதியில் அவசரகால நிலை அறிவிக்கப்பட்ட பின்னர் UF அரசாங்கத்தால் நசுக்கப்பட்டது. இருந்தபோதிலும், ஆளுங்கட்சி மீதான வெறுப்பு நீடித்தது. 1977 இல் பாராளுமன்றத் தேர்தல்கள் நடைபெற்றபோது, ​​ஜே.ஆர். ஜெயவர்த்தன தலைமையிலான ஐக்கிய தேசியக் கட்சி 168 ஆசனங்களில் 140 ஆசனங்களைப் பெற்று மீண்டும் ஆட்சிக்கு வந்தது. இந்த அதிபெரும்பான்மையைப் பயன்படுத்தி புதிய அரசியலமைப்பை உருவாக்கி தன்னை நிறைவேற்று ஜனாதிபதியாக - கிட்டத்தட்ட வரம்பற்ற அதிகாரங்களுடன் அபிஷேகம் செய்தார்.

ஜெயவர்த்தனவின் கீழ் சிங்கள மேலாதிக்கம் வேரூன்றியது, அவர் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்ட ஒரு மாதத்திற்குப் பிறகு தமிழர் விரோத படுகொலைகள் நாடு முழுவதும் பரவின. 1979 பயங்கரவாதத் தடைச் சட்டம் ஆயிரக்கணக்கான தமிழர்கள் சித்திரவதை, காணாமல் ஆக்கப்படுதல் மற்றும் சட்டத்திற்குப் புறம்பாக படுகொலை செய்யப்பட்டதை துரிதப்படுத்தியது. 1981 ஆம் ஆண்டில், யாழ்ப்பாணத்தில் அரச அனுசரணையுடன் நடத்தப்பட்ட தீக்குளிப்பு, கற்பழிப்பு மற்றும் சூறையாடலில் பொது நூலகம் எரிக்கப்பட்டது, அதில் சுமார் 95,000 புத்தகங்கள் மற்றும் பழங்கால கையெழுத்துப் பிரதிகள் இருந்தன. வன்முறை பின்னர் கிழக்கு, தெற்கு மற்றும் மலைப்பகுதிக்கு மாறியது, ஆயிரக்கணக்கான தமிழர்கள் தங்கள் வீடுகளில் இருந்து வெளியேற்றப்பட்டனர் மற்றும் அவர்களின் உடைமைகளை கொள்ளையடித்தனர். 1983 ஆம் ஆண்டு இன்னும் கொடூரமான படுகொலைகள் விடுதலைப் புலிகளுக்கும் அரசாங்கத்திற்கும் இடையில் உள்நாட்டுப் போரைத் தொடங்கின, அது சுமார் 26 ஆண்டுகள் நீடித்தது.

இவை அனைத்தும் ஜெயவர்த்தனேவின் முன்னோடிகளின் போக்கை நிலைநிறுத்தியது. ஆனால் அவரது ஆட்சியை வேறுபடுத்திக் காட்டியது அதன் பேரழிவு தரும் நவதாராளமயமாக்கல் திட்டம் மற்றும் வெட்கமற்ற சர்வாதிகாரம். 1980 களில் நுகர்வோர் பொருட்களின் உற்பத்தி, விவசாய மற்றும் கைத்தொழில், மலிவான இறக்குமதியால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டது, அதே நேரத்தில் இலங்கையில் முன்னர் கிடைக்காத ஆடம்பர பொருட்களின் இறக்குமதி அந்நிய செலாவணிக்கு வடிகால் சேர்க்கப்பட்டது. புலம்பெயர்ந்த தொழிலாளர்கள், தேயிலை ஏற்றுமதி, சுற்றுலா மற்றும் புதிய வெளிநாட்டு முதலீடுகள் ஆகியவற்றிலிருந்து பணம் அனுப்பப்படுவது, தாராளமான வரி விடுமுறைகள் மற்றும் உள்ளீடுகளின் கட்டணமில்லா இறக்குமதியின் காரணமாக இடைவெளியை நிரப்பத் தவறிவிட்டது. இது தற்போதைய பொருளாதார நெருக்கடிக்கு அடிப்படையாக அமைந்தது, வெளிநாட்டுக் கடனில் தங்கியிருப்பது அதிகரித்தது.

இதற்கிடையில், ஜெயவர்த்தனா அனைத்து கருத்து வேறுபாடுகளையும் நசுக்கவும் ஜனநாயகத்தை அணைக்கவும் முயன்றார். அவர் புதிதாக நிறுவப்பட்ட ஜாதிக சேவக சங்கமய (ஜேஎஸ்எஸ்) தமிழர் விரோத படுகொலைகளில் ஈடுபடுத்தப்பட்டது, அதே போல் எதிர்க்கட்சி ஆதரவாளர்கள், நீதிபதிகள், தொழிற்சங்கவாதிகள் மற்றும் வேலைநிறுத்தம் செய்யும் தொழிலாளர்களைக் கொன்றது, அனைத்தும் காவல்துறையின் கூட்டுறவுடன். இரண்டாவது ஜே.வி.பி கிளர்ச்சி, 1987 ஆம் ஆண்டின் நடுப்பகுதியில் தொடங்கி 1989 ஆம் ஆண்டின் பிற்பகுதியில் முடிவடைந்தது, விஜேவீர பிடிபட்டு தூக்கிலிடப்பட்டபோது, ​​அந்த காலகட்டத்தில் மட்டும் 40,000-60,000 சிங்களவர்கள் படுகொலை செய்யப்பட்டனர். ஜயவர்தன மற்றும் அவருக்குப் பின் வந்த ரணசிங்க பிரேமதாச ஆகியோரின் எதிரிகளை இலக்காகக் கொண்ட கொலைப் படைகள், அடிக்கடி கடத்திச் சென்று சித்திரவதை செய்து கொன்றனர். தற்போதைய இலங்கைப் பிரதமரான ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க இந்தக் காலகட்டம் முழுவதும் அரசாங்க அமைச்சராக இருந்தார். தற்போதைய ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ச ஒரு இராணுவ தளபதி. தமிழர்கள் மற்றும் சிங்களவர்கள் படுகொலை செய்யப்பட்டதில் இருவரும் சம்பந்தப்பட்டவர்கள்.

அப்போதிருந்து, நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறை தொடர்ச்சியான இழுபறிக்கு உட்படுத்தப்பட்டது. பாராளுமன்றத்தில் மூன்றில் இரண்டு பெரும்பான்மை மற்றும் ஒரு வாக்கெடுப்பில் முழுமையான பெரும்பான்மை தேவை என்று நீதிமன்றங்கள் தீர்ப்பளித்துள்ளதால், அதை ரத்து செய்வது இதுவரை மழுப்பலாக நிரூபிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. ஆயினும்கூட, ஜனாதிபதியின் அதிகாரத்தை கட்டுப்படுத்துவதற்கு மிகவும் எளிமையான சீர்திருத்தங்கள் எப்போதாவது நிறைவேற்றப்பட்டன. சந்திரிகா குமாரதுங்கவின் ஜனாதிபதியின் கீழ், 1994 முதல் 2005 வரை, ஜனநாயகத்தின் மீதான தாக்குதல்கள் குறைந்து, 17வது திருத்தம் கொண்டு வரப்பட்டது, தேர்தல் ஆணையம் மற்றும் உச்ச நீதிமன்றம் போன்ற நிறுவனங்களுக்கு ஒருதலைப்பட்சமாக ஆட்களை நியமிக்கும் ஜனாதிபதியின் திறனை நீக்கியது. அதிருப்தியாளர்களை குறிவைக்க அரச ஆதரவு கொலைப்படைகள் புத்துயிர் பெற்றதால், இந்த தற்காலிக முன்னேற்றம் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவின் கீழ் தலைகீழாக மாற்றப்பட்டது. 2009 இல், எல்.ரீ.ரீ.ஈ இறுதியாக உள்நாட்டுப் போரின் கொடூரமான உச்சக்கட்டத்தில் தோற்கடிக்கப்பட்டது, இதில் 40,000 தமிழ் பொதுமக்கள் கொல்லப்பட்டனர். இதனுடன் இணைந்து, 18வது அரசியலமைப்புத் திருத்தம் குமாரதுங்கவின் சீர்திருத்தங்களை மாற்றியமைத்தது மற்றும் ஜனாதிபதியின் இரண்டு கால வரம்புகளை நீக்கியது.

போர்க்குற்றங்கள் பற்றிய அறிக்கைகள் மீது சுதந்திரமான விசாரணை நடத்த வேண்டும் என்ற ஐ.நாவின் கோரிக்கையை ராஜபக்ச நிராகரித்து, இதை ஒரு ‘ஏகாதிபத்திய எதிர்ப்பு’ நிலைப்பாடு என்று வடிவமைத்தார். ஆனால் அவரது சொல்லாட்சி சில வாக்காளர்களுடன் நன்றாக விளையாடிய போதிலும், அவர் நாட்டின் வெளிநாட்டுக் கடனுக்கு பங்களிப்பதன் மூலம் நம்பகத்தன்மையை இழந்தார் - சீன அரசாங்கம் உட்பட புதிய கடன் வழங்குபவர்கள் மற்றும் இறையாண்மை பத்திரங்களை தனியார் வாங்குபவர்கள். அவர்களின் புகழ் வீழ்ச்சியடைந்த நிலையில், ஜனாதிபதியும் அவரது சகோதரர் கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷவும் - பாதுகாப்புச் செயலாளராக, புலனாய்வு அமைப்புகளைக் கட்டுப்படுத்தியவர் - முஸ்லிம் மக்களைப் பலிகடா ஆக்கி தங்கள் வாழ்க்கையைக் காப்பாற்ற முயன்றனர்.

அவர்கள் பௌத்த துறவிகளின் தீவிர வலதுசாரி குழுக்களுக்கு நிதியுதவி செய்தனர், அவர்களைப் பயன்படுத்தி முஸ்லிம்களை வன்முறையில் தாக்கினர், அதே நேரத்தில் அரசு கட்டுப்பாட்டில் உள்ள ஊடகங்கள் மூலம் இஸ்லாமிய வெறுப்பு பிரச்சாரத்தை மேற்கொண்டனர். ரேடாரின் கீழ், ராஜபக்சேக்கள் விடுதலைப் புலிகளுக்கு எதிராகப் போரிட இஸ்லாமியப் போராளிகளுக்கு நிதியுதவி செய்தனர் - அவர்கள் தீவிரமயமாக்கப்பட்டதாக நம்பத்தகுந்த உளவுத்துறை இருந்தபோதிலும், அவர்கள் அரசாங்கத்தின் சம்பளப் பட்டியலில் தகவல் கொடுப்பவர்களாக இருந்தனர்.

மகிந்த ராஜபக்ச 2015 இல் பதவியில் இருந்து வெளியேற்றப்பட்டார், அவரது ஆட்சியின் அவதூறான உறவுமுறை மற்றும் ஊழல் மூலம் சிங்கள மக்களின் பெரும் பகுதியினரை அந்நியப்படுத்தினார். அவருக்குப் பதிலாக, சிறுபான்மை இனத்தவர்கள் SLFP கிளர்ச்சியாளர் மைத்திரிபால சிறிசேன மற்றும் ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்க ஆகியோருக்கு இடையிலான பலவீனமான நல்லாட்சி (Yahapalanaya) கூட்டணிக்கு வாக்களித்தனர்.

புதிய கூட்டணி மீண்டும் ஜனாதிபதியின் அதிகாரங்களைக் குறைத்ததுடன், 19வது திருத்தத்தின் மூலம் இரண்டு கால எல்லையை மீண்டும் நிலைநிறுத்தியது. ஆனால் அதுவும் உடைந்து விழுந்தது. 2019 இல் ஈஸ்டர் ஞாயிறு பயங்கரவாதத் தாக்குதல் அதன் நம்பகத்தன்மைக்கு இறுதி அடியாகும், இது நாடு முழுவதும் உள்ள இடங்களில் 269 பேரைக் கொன்றது. ராஜபக்சேக்கள் பணப்பட்டுவாடா செய்த அதே இஸ்லாமியர்களால்தான் குண்டுவெடிப்புகளும் நடந்தன என்பது தெரிய வந்தது. யஹபாலனய ஆட்சியின் போது, ​​மூளைச்சாவடைந்த சஹ்ரான் ஹாசிம் உள்ளிட்ட பயங்கரவாதக் குழு உறுப்பினர்களுக்கு, கோட்டாபயவுக்கு விசுவாசமாக இருந்த அதிகாரிகளால், தொடர்ந்து பணம் வழங்கப்பட்டு, வழக்குகளில் இருந்து பாதுகாத்து வந்ததும் அடுத்தடுத்த விசாரணைகளில் தெரியவந்துள்ளது. ஹஷிம் ISIS க்கு விசுவாசமாக இருப்பதாக பிரகடனம் செய்த போதிலும், அவரைப் பின்பற்றுபவர்கள் ஆயுதங்கள் மற்றும் வெடிபொருட்களைக் குவித்ததற்கான ஏராளமான ஆதாரங்கள் இருந்தபோதிலும் இது நடந்தது.

ஆயினும், எதிர் மறையாக, ஈஸ்டர் ஞாயிறு படுகொலையில் பலனடைந்தவர்கள் ராஜபக்சக்களே. இதன் விளைவாக ஏற்பட்ட பீதியில், அரசாங்கம் பலவீனமடைந்தது மற்றும் 'தேசிய பாதுகாப்பு' வேட்பாளராக போட்டியிட்டு, கோத்தபய ஒரு பயனுள்ள ஜனாதிபதி பிரச்சாரத்தை மேற்கொள்ள முடிந்தது. அந்த ஆண்டின் பிற்பகுதியில், அவர் 52% வாக்குகளைப் பெற்று முதலிடம் பிடித்தார். ராஜபக்சவின் புதிய கட்சியான ஸ்ரீலங்கா பொதுஜன பெரமுனா தலைமையிலான கூட்டணி, 19வது திருத்தத்தை மாற்றி, 20வது திருத்தத்தை நிறைவேற்ற போதுமான பெரும்பான்மையுடன் நாடாளுமன்றத் தேர்தலில் வெற்றி பெற்றது. பிரதமராக நியமிக்கப்பட்ட மகிந்த உள்ளிட்ட குடும்ப உறுப்பினர்களுடன் கோட்டாபய அமைச்சரவையை கூட்டிச் சென்றார்.

இந்த நேரத்தில் பொருளாதாரம் ஏற்கனவே $51 பில்லியன் வெளிநாட்டுக் கடனின் கீழ் மூழ்கிக் கொண்டிருந்தது, இதில் பெரும்பகுதி குடும்பத்தின் வேனிட்டி திட்டங்கள் மற்றும் முடிவில்லாத பணத்தை நாட்டிலிருந்து வெளியேற்றியது. கோட்டாபய ஆட்சியின் வரிக் குறைப்புக்கள் கடனைத் தாங்க முடியாததாக ஆக்கியது மற்றும் இரசாயன உர இறக்குமதிக்கு ஒரே இரவில் தடை - விவசாயிகளின் எதிர்ப்புகளை எதிர்கொண்டு செயல்படுத்தப்பட்டது - பயிர் விளைச்சலில் பெரும் சரிவுக்கு வழிவகுத்தது. அன்னிய கையிருப்பு தீர்ந்ததால், உணவு மற்றும் ஏற்றுமதியின் உள்நாட்டு உற்பத்தி சரிந்து, வேலையில்லாத் திண்டாட்டம், விண்ணைத் தொடும் பணவீக்கம், மின்வெட்டு மற்றும் அடிப்படைப் பொருட்களை வாங்க நீண்ட வரிசையில் நிற்க வழிவகுத்தது.

பெரும் பணக்காரர்களைத் தவிர மற்ற அனைவரும் இந்த உருக்கத்தால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளனர். தொழிலாளர்கள் வேலையிழந்துள்ளனர், விவசாயிகள் நெருக்கடியில் உள்ளனர், மீனவர்கள் தங்கள் படகுகளை இயக்குவதற்கு எரிபொருள் இல்லை. அதிகரித்து வரும் பணவீக்கம் ஊதியத்தை அரித்துள்ளது, மேலும் பெற்றோர்கள் தங்கள் குழந்தைகளுக்கு உணவளிப்பதற்காக பட்டினி கிடக்கிறார்கள். எனவே, 2022 மார்ச் மாத தொடக்கத்தில், அனைத்து இன சமூகங்களைச் சேர்ந்த அனைத்து வயதினரும், ‘ராஜபக்ஷே வீட்டுக்குப் போங்கள்’ போன்ற வாசகங்களைத் தாங்கிய வீடுகளில் தயாரிக்கப்பட்ட பதாகைகளுடன் தன்னிச்சையாக வெளியே வந்தனர். ஜனநாயக சீர்திருத்தம், ஜனாதிபதி மற்றும் அவரது அரசாங்கம் உடனடியாக ராஜினாமா செய்ய வேண்டும் மற்றும் பொருளாதார தவறான நிர்வாகத்திற்கு முடிவு கட்ட வேண்டும் என்று அவர்கள் அழைப்பு விடுத்தனர்.

அரசாங்கம் இந்த ஆரம்ப எதிர்ப்புக்களை புறக்கணித்தது, ஆனால் மார்ச் இறுதியில் கொழும்பில் உள்ள கோட்டாபய இராஜபக்ஷவின் இல்லத்திற்கு அருகில் மேலும் ஒரு போர்க்குணமிக்க ஆர்ப்பாட்டம் தண்ணீர் பீரங்கி, கண்ணீர் புகை குண்டுகள் மற்றும் டஜன் கணக்கான கைதுகளை சந்தித்தது.

அவசரகாலச் சட்டம் மற்றும் நாடு தழுவிய ஊரடங்கு உத்தரவு மற்றும் சமூக ஊடகத் தடை ஆகியவற்றைத் தொடர்ந்து. இந்த கடுமையான பதில் அமைதியின்மையை அகற்றும் என்று எதிர்பார்க்கப்பட்டது, ஆனால் அது பரவுவதற்கு மட்டுமே உதவியது. ஜனாதிபதி தனது எதேச்சதிகார நடவடிக்கைகளை மாற்றியமைத்து, முழு அமைச்சரவையையும் (மகிந்தவைத் தவிர) ராஜினாமா செய்ய நிர்ப்பந்தித்து ஆர்ப்பாட்டக்காரர்களை சமாதானப்படுத்த முயன்றார்.

ஆனால் எதிர்ப்புக்கள் அவற்றின் வேகத்தைத் தொடர்ந்தன, ஏப்ரல் 9 அன்று ஆர்வலர்கள் காலி முகத்திடலை ஆக்கிரமித்தனர்: கொழும்பில் ஜனாதிபதி செயலகத்தை எதிர்கொள்ளும் பூங்கா. இந்த சின்னமான தளம் 'GotaGoGama' என மறுபெயரிடப்பட்டுள்ளது. 1,000 வெவ்வேறு தொழிற்சங்கங்களின் பிரதிநிதிகள் கூட்டத்துடன் இணைந்தனர், அவர்கள் ஒரு பொது வேலைநிறுத்தத்தை நடத்தினர் - நான்கு தசாப்தங்களில் முதல் - அரசாங்கம் பதவி விலக வேண்டும் என்று அழைப்பு விடுத்தது.

ஒரு மாதத்திற்குப் பிறகு, அரசு சார்பு குண்டர்கள் போராட்டங்கள் மீது வன்முறைத் தாக்குதல்களை நடத்தத் தொடங்கினர். ஆனாலும் அவர்களின் எதிர்ப்பு சக்தி வாய்ந்ததாக இருந்ததால், இறுதியில் மஹிந்த பதவி விலக வேண்டிய கட்டாயம் ஏற்பட்டது. பார்த்தவுடன் சுடும் உத்தரவுகளுடன் ராணுவம் நிறுத்தப்பட்ட நிலையில், பாதுகாப்புப் படையினரால் அவர் தனது வீட்டிலிருந்து வெளியேற்றப்பட்டார். அரசாங்கத்தின் மீதான சர்வதேச விமர்சனங்கள் அதிகரித்து வரும் நிலையில், ஐக்கிய தேசியக் கட்சியின் தலைவரான ரணில் விக்கிரமசிங்கவை மே 12 அன்று கோட்டாபய பிரதமராக நியமித்தார். ஆனால் விக்கிரமசிங்க சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியத்தில் பிரபலமாக இருந்தாலும், அவர் வெகுஜனங்களால் ஆழமாக வெறுக்கப்படுகிறார். அவரது முன்மொழியப்பட்ட 21 வது திருத்தம் எதிர்ப்பாளர்களின் கோரிக்கைகளுக்கு துரோகம் செய்வதாக பரவலாகக் கருதப்படுகிறது, மேலும் இளைஞர் குழுக்களை பாராளுமன்றக் குழுக்களில் அமருமாறு அவர் அழைப்பு விடுத்தது அதற்குத் தகுதியான மௌனத்தை சந்தித்துள்ளது.

நிறைவேற்று அதிகாரம் கொண்ட ஜனாதிபதி முறைமையை நீக்குவதற்கு ஆதரவான எண்ணிக்கை தற்போது கோட்டாபயவின் இராஜினாமாவை விட குறைவாகவே உள்ளது, ஆனால் கோரிக்கை வலுப்பெற்று வருகிறது. இது செயல்பாட்டாளர்களுக்கு ஒரு திறப்பை உருவாக்குகிறது, அவர்கள் இப்போது மாகாண மற்றும் உள்ளூர் அரசாங்கங்களுக்கு அதிகாரத்தைப் பகிர்ந்தளிக்கும் அரசியல் மறுசீரமைப்பின் பரந்த செயல்முறைக்கு அழுத்தம் கொடுக்க முடியும். நாட்டின் பொருளாதார நெருக்கடிக்கு முற்போக்கான தீர்வுகளுக்கான இடத்தையும் இது வழங்குகிறது.

சர்வதேச நாணய நிதியத்தை மீறி, இலங்கையின் சட்டவிரோதக் கடனை நிராகரிக்கும் பொதுக் கணக்காய்வுக்கு சோசலிசப் பொருளாதார வல்லுநர்கள் நீண்டகாலமாக வாதிடுகின்றனர். உணவு மற்றும் மருந்து போன்ற அத்தியாவசியப் பொருட்களை மட்டுமே இறக்குமதி செய்து பொது விநியோக முறையை நடைமுறைப்படுத்த வேண்டும் என்று அவர்கள் வாதிட்டனர், அதே நேரத்தில் கூட்டுறவு உற்பத்தியாளர்களை ஊக்குவித்தல் மற்றும் பயன்பாடுகள், சுகாதாரம் மற்றும் கல்வி ஆகியவற்றின் பொது உரிமையைப் பாதுகாக்க வேண்டும்.

கோட்டகோகமவில், சிங்களவர்களும் தமிழர்களும் இணைந்து புத்தாண்டைக் கொண்டாடியதாகவும், பல்வேறு மதக் குழுக்களும் ரமழானின் நோன்பு துறப்பதில் பங்குகொண்டதாகவும் கூறப்படுகிறது. இதேவேளை, உள்நாட்டுப் போரில் கொல்லப்பட்ட தமிழர்களுக்கு இரங்கல் தெரிவிக்க முதல் முறையாக தென்னிலங்கையில் மக்கள் குவிந்துள்ளனர். இத்தகைய அபிவிருத்திகள் இன மற்றும் மதப் பதட்டங்களைத் தணிக்க ராஜபக்சக்கள் முயற்சித்த போதிலும், அவற்றைத் தணிக்கச் செய்வதை அறிவுறுத்துகின்றன.

1993 ஆம் ஆண்டு எனது இலக்கற்ற பயணம் என்ற புத்தகத்திற்கு நான் நேர்காணல்களை நடத்தும் போது, ​​தமிழ் மற்றும் முஸ்லிம் அகதிகள் மற்றும் இடம்பெயர்ந்த மக்களில் பெரும்பாலோர் இன வெறுப்பு இல்லாமல் போற்றத்தக்க வகையில் அவர்கள் துன்பங்களை அனுபவித்தனர். சிங்கள நண்பர்கள், அயலவர்கள், சகாக்கள் மற்றும் அந்நியர்கள் கூட தமிழர்களின் உயிரைக் காப்பாற்றிய பல கதைகளை நான் கேள்விப்பட்டிருக்கிறேன்.

குறிப்பாக சிங்களவர்களிடையே நான் தப்பெண்ணத்தை எதிர்கொண்டேன், ஆயினும் இது சிங்களம் மட்டும் கொள்கையால் உருவான ஆழமான அறியாமை, அத்துடன் அதிருப்திக் குரல்கள் மற்றும் ஊடகங்களில் இடைவிடாத தவறான தகவல்களை அடக்குதல் ஆகியவற்றிலிருந்து பாய்ந்தது. அனுபவம் பிரச்சாரத்திற்கு முரணானபோது, ​​​​மக்கள் பெரும்பாலும் புதிதாக சிந்திக்கத் தயாராக இருந்தனர். மேலும் இதுவே தற்போது நடக்கிறது. ஒருவேளை, இலங்கையின் உடைந்த இடதுசாரிகள் இந்த ஒற்றுமை உணர்வைப் பயன்படுத்தினால், பொருளாதாரப் பேரழிவு ஒரு ஜனநாயக முன்னேற்றத்திற்கான சூழ்நிலையை உருவாக்கலாம்.

en.title: The prevailing  economic catastrophe may create the conditions for a democratic breakthrough,  if Sri Lanka’s fractured left could harness this  emerging sense of solidarity among Affected people
body_lines: I am not in Sri Lanka, and I feel torn about what is happening there. Acute anxiety about how millions will survive the shortages of food, fuel and medicine jostles against a glimmer of hope that this crisis could be the beginning of the end of a decades-long nightmare.

Since the country gained its Independence in 1948, various sections of the population have been targeted by its ruling bloc: threatened with losing their homes, livelihoods and often their lives. They have fought back, but each section has been isolated and crushed by an increasingly centralized and ruthless state. Now, for the first time, the vast majority of the population has risen in revolt. Criticism of the dictatorship is widespread, and divisions between working people may finally be healing.

It is not easy to disentangle the different strands of Sri Lanka’s long-standing political crisis, but let me try. Equality before the law – a key component of any democratic republic – was never supported by the ruling class that took power in independent Ceylon. The two main parties, the United National Party (UNP) and the Sri Lanka Freedom Party (SLFP), both endorsed ‘Sinhala-Buddhist’ supremacism. This meant persistent discrimination against ethno-religious minorities, which began right after Independence, when the UNP passed legislation disenfranchising around a million Tamils of recent Indian origin and stripping them of their citizenship. Most of those affected were plantation workers in the central hill country, who were already isolated from other sections of the working class by their confinement to the plantations.

The next major assault on equality occurred when the SLFP, led by S.W.R.D. Bandaranaike, came to power in 1956 and passed the Official Language Act, or ‘Sinhala Only Bill’. The Act discriminated against all Tamil-speakers, especially in public sector employment. It sparked major protests followed by the anti-Tamil riots of 1958, in which far-right Buddhist monks played a major part, assassinating Bandaranaike the following year for not going far enough in persecuting Tamils. The leadership of the SLFP was taken over by his widow Sirimavo Bandaranaike. Tamil was downgraded and English ceased to act as a link language, in a deliberate attempt to obstruct dialogue between communities.

Such measures were opposed by the Trotskyist Lanka Sama Samaja Party (LSSP) and the Communist Party of Ceylon (later the Communist Party of Sri Lanka, CPSL). Yet, once they failed to prevent the ratification of the anti-Tamil laws, the left parties did not continue the struggle against discrimination by building solidarity among working people from different linguistic and religious groups.

Instead, they formed an alliance with the SLFP in 1964, and the parties jointly established the United Front (UF), which swept to power in 1970. At that point, principled members of the left parties split off, and Tamil socialists were left demoralized by the capitulation of their leaders. The only force that could have carried through the democratic revolution had splintered.

Once in power, the UF’s Land Reform Laws of 1972 and 1975 nationalized the plantations. Yet rather than distributing the land to Tamil workers – who were driven out and left to starve – it was handed to Sinhalese government supporters. In response, the Tamil United Liberation Front (TULF) called for the creation of an independent Tamil Eelam. Militant groups, most notably the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (LTTE), were established to fight for this goal.

The LTTE initially attracted some Tamil socialists who believed it was waging a justified struggle for self-determination; but, in reality, the group was always committed to creating a Tamil-supremacist state by ethnically cleansing and killing Sinhalese. It even targeted Tamil-speaking Muslims in the Northern and Eastern Provinces which it claimed as its territory.

Despite all the privileges given to the Sinhalese majority by the UF, dissatisfaction with the regime remained widespread. Significant gains in healthcare and education were cancelled out in the public mind by inflation and food shortages. In 1971, the Janatha Vimukthi Peramuna (JVP or People’s Liberation Front), led by Rohana Wijeweera, launched an armed uprising to overthrow the government – backed overwhelmingly by Sinhalese young men for whom the problems of unemployment and poverty had not been solved by Sinhala Only.

Notwithstanding his self-description as a ‘modern Bolshevik’, Wijeweera’s revolutionary horizons were narrowed by a Sinhala-supremacist outlook which characterized Tamil plantation workers as mere tools of Indian expansionism. His uprising was powerless to reach across ethnic lines, and ultimately crushed by the UF government after a state of emergency was declared. Nonetheless, antipathy towards the ruling party lingered. When parliamentary elections were held in 1977, the UNP led by J.R. Jayawardene returned to power with 140 seats out of 168. He used this super-majority to enact a new constitution and anoint himself Executive President – with almost unlimited powers.

Sinhala dominance was entrenched under Jayawardene, with anti-Tamil pogroms sweeping the country just a month after his election. The 1979 Prevention of Terrorism Act precipitated the torture, disappearance and extrajudicial killing of thousands of Tamils. In 1981, an orgy of state-sponsored arson, rape and looting in Jaffna included the burning of the public library, with around 95,000 books and ancient manuscripts inside. The violence then shifted to the east, south and hill-country, with thousands of Tamils evicted from their homes and robbed of their possessions. The even more gruesome massacres of 1983 initiated a civil war between the LTTE and government, which lasted some 26 years.

All this perpetuated the trend set by Jayawardene’s predecessors. But what distinguished his regime was its disastrous neoliberalization programme and unabashed authoritarianism. Production of consumer goods, both agricultural and industrial, was hit by cheaper imports in the 1980s, while the import of luxuries previously unavailable in Sri Lanka added to the drain on foreign exchange. Remittances from migrant workers, tea exports, tourism and new foreign investments failed to fill the gap, due to generous tax holidays and tariff-free imports of inputs. This led to increasing reliance on foreign debt, laying the basis for the current economic crisis.

Meanwhile, Jayawardene attempted to crush all dissent and extinguish democracy. His newly established Jathika Sevaka Sangamaya (JSS) was deployed in anti-Tamil pogroms, as well as killing opposition supporters, judges, trade unionists and striking workers, all with the collusion of the police. The second JVP insurrection, starting in mid-1987 and ending in late 1989, when Wijeweera was captured and executed, left an estimated 40,000-60,000 Sinhalese slaughtered in that period alone. Death squads targeted opponents of Jayawardene and his successor Ranasinghe Premadasa, frequently abducting and torturing them to death. Ranil Wickremesinghe, the current Sri Lankan Prime Minister, was a government minister throughout this period. The current president, Gotabaya Rajapaksa, was an army commander. Both were implicated in the mass murder of Tamils and Sinhalese alike.

Since that period, the Executive Presidency has been subjected to an ongoing tug-of-war. Abolishing it has so far proved elusive, because the courts have ruled that this would require a two-thirds majority in parliament plus an absolute majority in a referendum. Yet more modest reforms have occasionally been passed to restrict the presidency’s power. Under the presidency of Chandrika Kumaratunga, from 1994 to 2005, attacks on democracy declined and the 17th Amendment was instituted, removing the president’s ability to unilaterally appoint people to institutions like the Election Commission and Supreme Court. This tentative progress was then reversed under Mahinda Rajapaksa, as state-backed death squads were revived to target dissidents. In 2009, the LTTE was finally defeated in the civil war’s horrific climax, in which an estimated 40,000 Tamil civilians were killed. In tandem, an 18th constitutional amendment reversed Kumaratunga’s reforms and abolished the presidency’s two-term limit.

Rajapaksa rejected the UN’s demand for an independent investigation into reports of war crimes, framing this as an ‘anti-imperialist’ position. But though his rhetoric played well with some of the electorate, he lost credibility by contributing to the country’s mountain of foreign debt – with new creditors including the Chinese government and private buyers of sovereign bonds. With their popularity in decline, the president and his brother Gotabaya Rajapaksa – who, as Defence Secretary, controlled the intelligence agencies – tried to salvage their careers by scapegoating the Muslim population.

They funded far-right groups of Buddhist monks, using them to violently attack Muslims while mounting an Islamophobic propaganda campaign through state-controlled media. Under the radar, the Rajapaksas also funded Islamist militants to fight against the LTTE – who remained on the government payroll as informants despite credible intelligence that they had been radicalized.

Mahinda Rajapaksa was voted out of office in 2015, having alienated a large section of the Sinhalese population with the scandalous nepotism and corruption of his regime. In his place, ethnic minorities voted for a fragile Good Governance (Yahapalanaya) coalition between SLFP rebel Maithripala Sirisena and Ranil Wickremesinghe.

The new coalition once again curtailed the powers of the president and reinstated the two-term limit with the 19th Amendment. But it, too, fell apart. The final blow to its credibility was the Easter Sunday terrorist attack in 2019, which killed 269 people in locations across the country. As it turned out, the bombings were perpetrated by the very same Islamists that the Rajapaksas had been bankrolling. Subsequent investigations revealed that during the Yahapalanaya regime, members of the terror group, including mastermind Zahran Hashim, continued to be paid and protected from prosecution by officials who remained loyal to Gotabaya. This was despite Hashim’s proclamation of allegiance to ISIS and ample evidence that his followers were accumulating arms and explosives.

Yet, counterintuitively, it was the Rajapaksas who benefited from the Easter Sunday massacre. In the resulting panic, the government was weakened and Gotabaya was able to mount an effective presidential campaign, running as the ‘national security’ candidate. Later that year, he topped the poll with 52% of the vote. An alliance led by the Rajapaksas’ new party, the Sri Lanka Podujana Peramuna, also won the parliamentary elections with a large enough majority to pass the 20th Amendment, reversing the 19th. Gotabaya proceeded to pack the cabinet with family members including Mahinda, who was appointed prime minister.

By this time the economy was already sinking under $51 billion of foreign debt, much of it incurred by the family’s vanity projects and endless siphoning of money out of the country. The Gotabaya regime’s tax cuts made the debt unsustainable, and an overnight ban on imports of chemical fertiliser – implemented in the face of farmer protests – led to a colossal decline in crop yields. As foreign reserves ran out, domestic production of food and exports plummeted, leading to escalating unemployment, sky-rocketing inflation, power cuts and long queues to buy basic goods.

All but the very rich have been affected by this meltdown. Workers have lost jobs, farmers are in crisis and fishermen have no fuel to power their boats. Galloping inflation has eroded wages, and parents have gone hungry in order to feed their children. So, in early March 2022, people of all ages, from all ethnic communities, came out spontaneously with home-made placards bearing slogans such as ‘Go Home Rajapaksas’. They called for democratic reform, the immediate resignation of the president and his government, and an end to economic mismanagement.

The government ignored these initial protests, but at the end of March a more militant demonstration near Gotabaya Rajapksa’s residence in Colombo was met with water cannons, tear gas and dozens of arrests.

There followed a state of emergency, plus a nationwide curfew and social media ban. This heavy-handed response was expected to stamp out the unrest, but it only enabled it to spread. The president subsequently changed tack, trying to appease the demonstrators by reversing his authoritarian measures and forcing the entire cabinet (apart from Mahinda) to resign.

But the protests kept up their momentum, and on 9 April activists occupied Galle Face Green: a park in Colombo facing the Presidential Secretariat. This now iconic site has been renamed ‘GotaGoGama’. The crowds were joined by a delegation from 1,000 different trade unions, who staged a general strike – the first in four decades – calling for the government to step down.

A month later, pro-government thugs began to carry out violent attacks on the protests. Yet their resistance was so powerful that Mahinda was ultimately compelled to step down. He was evacuated from his home by security forces while the military was deployed with shoot-on-sight orders. With international criticism of the government growing, Gotabaya installed Ranil Wickremesinghe – the leader of the United National Party – as Prime Minister on 12 May. But although Wickremesinghe may be popular with the IMF, he is deeply disliked by the masses. His proposed 21st Amendment has been widely seen as a betrayal of the protesters’ demands, and his invitation to youth groups to sit on parliamentary committees has been met with the silence it deserves.

The numbers in favour of abolishing the Executive Presidency are currently smaller than those calling for Gotabaya’s resignation, but the demand is gaining traction. This creates an opening for activists, who can now push for a broader process of political restructuring which would devolve power to provincial and local governments. It also provides a space for progressive solutions to the country’s economic crisis.

Socialist economists have long advocated a public audit that would repudiate Sri Lanka’s illegitimate debt, in defiance of the IMF. They have argued for importing only essential items like food and medicine and putting in place a public distribution system, while encouraging cooperative producers and defending public ownership of utilities, healthcare and education.

At GotaGoGama, Sinhalese and Tamils have reportedly celebrated New Year together, and various religious groups have shared in the breaking of the fast during Ramadan. Meanwhile, in the south, people have turned out for the very first time to mourn the Tamils killed in the civil war. Such developments suggest an easing of ethnic and religious tensions, despite the Rajapaksas attempts to stoke them.

When I was conducting interviews for my 1993 book, Journey Without a Destination, the vast majority of Tamil and Muslim refugees and displaced people were admirably free of ethnic hatred, despite all they had suffered. I heard numerous stories of Sinhalese friends, neighbours, colleagues and even total strangers saving the lives of Tamils.

I also encountered prejudice, especially among the Sinhalese, yet this flowed from the profound ignorance engendered by the Sinhala Only policy, as well as the suppression of dissident voices and relentless disinformation in the media. When experience contradicted propaganda, though, people were often willing to think anew. And this is precisely what is happening at present. Perhaps, if Sri Lanka’s fractured left can harness this sense of solidarity, the economic catastrophe may create the conditions for a democratic breakthrough.
